title: mercurialis annua var. ambigua
intro: Mercurialis annua var. ambigua, also known as the annual mercury or lesser mercury, is a plant species in the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae) that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Mercurialis annua var. ambigua include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
